title: region — retention windows
description: Region rows follow a soft-delete → archived → hard-delete lifecycle. The hold window is 730 days (2 years) matching financial-record retention. Audit rows produced by this module outlive their source Region for 7 years (financial retention floor).

cold.days: region.retention.archived_regions_retention_days (default 730 — 2 years; up to 2555 per Enterprise tenant setting)
cold.state: status=archived (soft-deleted, deleted_at set); hidden from tenant selectors + booking flows; readable by admins via `?include_archived=true`; inbound rows continue to point at it during the window.

purge.trigger: deleted_at + region.retention.archived_regions_retention_days past AND no inbound row references
purge.note: IF any inbound row (Branch, Membership, Invoice, Transaction) still references the Region past the retention window, the job SKIPS the row + logs a telemetry counter (`stackra.region.purge_pending.count`). The row survives another day. Explicit `region:archive --force` bypasses the FK check but does NOT delete referring rows.

cold.days: up to 2555 (7 years — financial retention floor)

purge.action: audit rows OUTLIVE their Region — that's the compliance signal for financial audits. Retention is set by `audit::config.retention_days` clamped up by the compliance floor for financial records (7 years).

purge.trigger: TTL expiry OR invalidation on Region mutation

notes: The 730-day hold window is the DEFAULT. Enterprise tenants may extend up to 2555 days (7 years) via the tenant setting — matching the audit-row retention so archived Regions and their audits both stay recoverable through the same window.

Hard-delete of an in-use Region is IMPOSSIBLE by design. The RegionObserver + FK RESTRICT combine to keep the Region alive as long as any Branch / Membership / Invoice / Transaction references it. This is intentional — a Region hard-delete would invalidate financial reconciliation queries that JOIN through region_id.
The `region_time_travel` entitlement (Enterprise-only, Wave 3) does NOT bypass retention. It enables retroactive tax_config edits on active Regions — it never accelerates purge.
